h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: 0.04% still feels like thin arithmetic to hang climate-scale claims on, even with that meter blinking at 420.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the framing problem was that I was picturing a room percentage, not a whole exit path to space.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to reject the 0.04% concentration as too small to have any physical impact on climate-scale heat flow.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair, or at least that was the standard in my head.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seeks to define a procedural line for lock-time adjustments while holding onto their initial standard of exactness.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was the thing to trust because Greenland looks almost absurdly large up there.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to reinforce the correct answer about Mercator projection distortion without looking back at their initial misconception.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think the ward sentence only licenses, sorry, the pattern across admissions in Ward C, not a claim about this patient.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to incrementally refine the cautiousness of the phrasing for the case summary.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the steel is not the bridge; the route is the bridge
scored.statedInsightEvidence: “Route is bridge” was my shortcut, but I was still using it like a poster slogan
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to build directly on their previous assertion that their initial formulation 'still stands' by simply narrowing its application.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the person's body gets used to the antibiotic, so I wrote it wrong on the poster.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to focus on resolving their confusion about when bacteria mutate versus when they are already resistant.
